page: Microsoft Azure L64s_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Lsv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 64 vCPUs, derived from 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. The hardware configuration includes 512.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For storage, the instance is equipped with 15,359 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. No GPU accelerators are included.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high data throughput. The server achieved a top-tier rating in Geekbench multi-core performance and Passmark database operations. Static web serving throughput reached a top-tier level of 93.83 GB/sec. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench image processing also registered top-tier and strong performance categories. Single-core performance, measured via stress-ng, sits in the average tier, indicating that workloads benefit more from parallel execution across its 64 vCPUs. Memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 225.0 GB/sec for large block read-only operations.

From a resource density perspective, the L64s_v4 offers a high ratio of local storage to compute resources, making it a cost-efficient option for data-intensive workloads that require massive local disk capacity without the overhead of external storage attachments. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a balanced memory profile for large datasets. However, the lack of dedicated GPU hardware limits its efficiency for heavy deep learning training, though CPU-based LLM inference benchmarks show strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium models.

This server configuration is suited for large-scale database deployments, high-throughput web serving, and data warehousing applications that leverage its 15 TB of local storage. It is also appropriate for parallel software compilation, memory-intensive data processing, and multi-threaded compression tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure L64s_v4 is an x86_64 storage-optimized server from the Lsv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores), 512.0 GB of RAM, and 15,359 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving, and multi-core processing benchmarks, though single-core performance remains average. With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and massive local storage capacity, the L64s_v4 provides high resource density for data-intensive applications. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and parallel data processing workloads that do not require GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L64s_v4 is a storage-optimized server featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 15,359 GB local storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L64s_v4 is an Intel-based server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 15 TB local storage, optimized for database and multi-core workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-optimized server featuring sixty-four vCPUs and fifteen terabytes of local storage for intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C CPU at 4.2 GHz
512 GB of system memory providing 8 GB of RAM per core
15,359 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier benchmark performance in database operations and static web serving
Optimized for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and parallel processing
